amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The cAMP-PKA pathway in yeast senses glucose and regulates cell growth, stress response, and metabolism. High glucose activates Gpr1 GPCR and Ras1/2 GTPases, stimulating adenylyl cyclase (Cyr1) to produce cAMP. cAMP binds regulatory subunit Bcy1, releasing catalytic subunits Tpk1/2/3. Active PKA phosphorylates targets including Msn2/4 (stress response TFs - nuclear export), trehalase (glycogen mobilization), and ribosomal proteins (translation). Feedback includes Pde1/2 phosphodiesterases degrading cAMP.

name: cAMP-PKA Pathway (Glucose Sensing and Growth Control)
notes: Shows high AND gates (8) for PKA activation requirements and pathway integration.
scientificAccuracy: Based on extensive research on yeast PKA signaling and glucose sensing.

sources.title: Genes for the yeast cAMP-dependent protein kinase catalytic subunit: isolation, sequence, and disruption.
sources.authors: Toda, T., Cameron, S., Sass, P., Zoller, M., Scott, J. D., Wigler, M.

sources.title: The yeast adenylyl cyclase gene: structure, function, and relationship to mammalian adenylyl cyclases.
sources.authors: Toda, T., Sass, P., Wigler, M.

sources.title: Nutritional controls on growth in yeast.
